details.text: Derivative: عِوَج in the Arabic language is used both literally and figuratively. Its literal meaning is deviation and crookedness. In its figurative sense it refers to something that is complicated that is not very clear, that appears to have some flaw in it or confusion concerning it.
Derivative: هَيِّأَ comes from هَيْأَة which means a shape. هَيِّأَ is to create a shape. Figuratively it became known with the meaning to provide and create the circumstances.
Derivative: A straight way or guidance but also in the literal sense of finding a place for their sanctuary.
Derivative: أَوَى in Arabic means to join something and get together with it for the purpose of protection.
- مِن لَّدُنكَ means especially from you (referring to Allah). The use of لَّدُن indicates to closeness or close attachment.
Derivative: غِطاء means a veil or a cover
Derivative: نُزُلٌ refers to initial hospitality with the expectation that something greater awaits.
Derivative: حِوَلًا is from تَحْويل and means change.

Derivative: مِداد is another word for ink beside حِبْر. It comes from مَدُّ which means extension. The مِداد (ink) here stems from it being the extension from the hand, your thoughts and understanding, extending from you on to the paper. مِداد is also used to refer to a large amount of ink that is extensive.

Derivative: كَلِمات has a wider meaning than the words of Allah contained in the Qur’an and can include His instructions related to the creation and maintenance of creation, His knowledge and wisdoms etc.
- Literally to be struck on their ears but its meaning is that their hearing was taken away and they were unable to hear sound whilst sleeping.
Derivative: أَمَد can be used for both time and place but is used for something that is prolonged. A far away place can be referred to as أَمَد just as it can mean a long time.

Derivative: أَحْصَىٰ
- حَصى is a pebble and the Arabs used pebbles for counting and for that أَحْصَى and إٍحْصاء came to be used for counting but unlike عَدَّ (to count) it means counting alongside keeping record.

Derivative: حِزْب is one of the words for جَمَاعَة (group) but حِزْب has an element of harshness associated with it and can refer to a faction of an army because they’re tough. حِزْب also means a like-minded group and also means people who are united under a particular flag. حِزْبَيْنِ refers to how the youth split into two groups in disagreement concerning how long they were asleep for.

Derivative: فِتْيَةٌ
- Emphasis on the mention of them being فِتْيَةٌ (young people) is to emphasise their status and as an example for others.

- This represents an interjection in the middle of the story to make a point to highlight that We are going to narrate the story in truth (بِٱلْحَقِّ) i.e., with its true facts, and tied to purpose i.e., that which can be learned from it.

Derivative: شَطَطًا
- شَطَط means a huge exaggeration, crazy lies and something that is outrageous.

- We put firmness upon their hearts (i.e., strengthened their hearts). This phrase is used in the Qur’an when very difficult emotional situations were faced and without that strengthening from Allah, they would not stand their ground. An example is when Musa’s mother put him in the water.
- “When they stood up”: This is an indication of bravery as standing up to speak the true word to the authority is an act of bravery.

- This phrase combines two sentiments: هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ (these) indicates to your distancing of them due to their associating partners with Allah whilst قَوْمُنَا (our people) indicates to closeness and attachment, reflecting concern for them.

Derivative: مِّرْفَقًا
- مِرِفَق is a means of relaxation like comfortable bedding or place that you will be at ease and comfortable in. From رِفْق to recline, find comfort and relax or رَفِيق a friend or person who makes you feel relaxed and at ease.

Derivative: ٱعْتَزَلْتُمُوهُمْ
- عَزَلَ and اِعْتَزَلَ in Arabic means to step aside and avoid something. It also means to be detached, isolated and to cut yourself off from something or others.

- فَأْوُۥٓا۟ (seek refuge) is in the أَمْر (command) form but does not mention who made the command. It could refer to an adviser of theirs or from amongst them or to inspiration from Allah.
- يَنشُرْ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِ Allah says He will expand (يَنشُرْ) for you from His mercy even though the cave (ٱلْكَهْفِ) is something that is tight and narrow by its nature, employing an opposite meaning. It may appear to be tight and confined but it will be an opening and expansion of mercy.
details.arabic_part: فَأْوُۥٓا۟   إِلَى   ٱلْكَهْفِ   يَنشُرْ   لَكُمْ   رَبُّكُم   مِّن   رَّحْمَتِهِ

details.text: Derivative: فَجْوَة is any small opening or enclave between mountains

Derivative: تَزاوَرَ linked to زُور like the statement of Allah قول الزُّور (speech of Zoor) describing speech that has deviation or crookedness in it. زُور when you step to the side and curve outwards and here تَزاوَرَ indicates to the curving of the light of the sun and indirect light entering the cave from an angle.
Derivative: Someone who guides you through a valley or a journey.
Derivative: In Arabic they say قَرَضَ المكان which means to sidestep or avoid the place. Here it indicates to the sun avoiding them in the evening.
Derivative: رُقُودٌ is a light sleep and not heavy one.
Derivative: اِطَّلَعَ is from طُلوع in Arabic which is to rise like the rising of the sun. اِطَّلَعَ means to discover something after climbing up.

Derivative: وَصِيد literally meanings the closing and indicates here to a very narrow opening (of the cave) where it would be thought that it is blocked. The linguistic meaning of وَصْد is the sealing of something by something else.
Derivative: باسِطٌ from بَسَطَ means to stretch out.
Derivative: لَطِيف means subtlety and تَلَطَّفَ means to approach a matter carefully.
Derivative: لَّدُنْ is the same word as لَدَى but لَّدُنْ is used when مِنْ occurs otherwise لَدَى would be used. It indicates to possession with nearness and availability.

Derivative: بَأْسًا means difficulty, social or economic trouble and can also mean in the worst-case scenario war like حَرْب (war) but is wider than حَرْب in its meaning in the sense that it encompasses everything that surrounds war of troubles.

Derivative: قَيِّم – It remains up right and keeps straight and upright ensuring it. It also means something that maintains and holds other things up like foundations hold a building up. It means here that the Qur’an keeps them in line.
- the structure of This statement indicates to This intense war being unavoidable Due to the close attachment to Allah indicated by لَّدُنْ which attributes it especially to Him.

Derivative: Here مِلَّة (religion) is used instead of دِين because they viewed their religion to be a part of their people’s or community’s identity in the case where leaving the religion was equivalent to being a traitor to the nation.

Derivative: أَعْثَرْنَا
Root: ع ث ر – The saying: عُثْرَة القَدَمِ خَيْرُ مِن عُثْرَةِ اللِّسَانِ (The slip of the foot is better than the slip of the tongue) in the case where عُثْرَة means “slip”. أَعْثَرَ means to make someone or something slip whereupon We stumbled upon them (أَعْثَرْنَا).
Derivative: مَارَى – يُمارِي – مِراء: To argue about something the conclusions or consequences of which are hard to accept.
- رَجْمًۢا بِٱلْغَيْبِ throwing stones in the dark (or unseen) is a figure of speech in Arabic which is equivalent to making wild guesses.

- This is أُسْلُوب التَّعَجُّب (style of expressing astonishment or marvelling) in the Arabic language utilising the أَفْعِل بِهِ form with the meaning: How amazingly well He sees and how amazingly well He hears.
Derivative: لَحْد means a refuge, a space in which you have wriggle room. The meaning of مُلْتَحَدًا here means that you will not even be able to find a space that you can wriggle out from.
Derivative: الغَداة is the third portion of the morning referring to the later morning.
Derivative: In excess, over the top and excessive in what he does and says.
Derivative: عَدا – يَعْدو – It means to cross a line (to bypass/go beyond).
Derivative: أَطاعَ – يُطِيعُ – To follow and also to be influenced by.
Derivative: ٱلْعَشِىِّ is the time just prior to Maghrib as the sun is setting.
Derivative: A place to recline and relax.
Derivative: سُرَادِق is a tent that doesn’t have a roof and also something that surrounds something like a wall.
Derivative: Like the yellow pus that comes out of a corpse or the yellow formation of melted copper – It refers to a liquid that is disgusting (putrid) or frightening.
Derivative: شَوَى – يَشْوِي means to roast (scald).

Derivative: مَكَثَ means to remain somewhere in anticipation of something. ماكثين adds the element of anticipation in relation to remaining in Jannah for eternity where there will be a continuous source of anticipation of its wonders and for more.
Derivative: Originally Persian. It means light silk.
Derivative: It refers to heavy silk.
Derivative: حَفَّ means to surround something from both sides.
- كِلْتَا ٱلْجَنَّتَيْنِ – This does not just mean “both” gardens but rather the “entirety” of the two gardens.
Derivative: نَفَرًا in this context refers to manpower and workers.

Derivative: From بَادَ – يَبيدُ – بَياداً: Means destruction and perishing and specifically in relation to the أَرْض (land/ground) like when the land and plants dry up and earth becomes barren. The death of the land.
Derivative: مُنقَلَبًا means in return i.e., in compensation.

Derivative: The origin of لَّٰكِنَّا۠ is لَكِن + أَنا – But I … It can indicate to the extreme humbleness of the one saying it in the case where he is unable to pronounce the whole word أَنا (I) and just says نا.
Derivative: صَعِيد is something that just gathers dust.
Derivative: حُسْبَانًا means a timely destruction and Al-Baydawiy argues that it means a lightning strike that creates a wildfire.
Derivative: زَلَق is something slippery indicating here to the land being so dry (and barren) that you just slip on it.
Derivative: غَوْرًا means for water to be sucked into the ground.

- To be turned on top or tipped over upon its trellises. عُرُوش are the sticks and wooden frames that the grape vine wraps around. (An expression indicating to something falling into ruin and destruction).
Derivative: فِئَةٌ is a group that provides help and assistance.
Derivative: وَلَايَة means love, aid and support or protective help (نُصْرَة).
Derivative: تَذْرُوهُ from ذَرَا – يَذْرُو which means to throw up in the air.
Derivative: اخْتَلَطَ to mix, when a thing joins to another thing.
Derivative: هَشِيم means to be defeated, shred to pieces and torn up. Here in the context of crops it means that they dried up and died.
- the remaining good deeds referring to those deeds that last.
Derivative: حَشَرَ means to herd (with the meaning of to gather). حَشَرَ is used for gathering animals (i.e., to herd them) and جَمَعَ is used for humans.
Derivative: سَارَ – يَسيرُ means to walk casually. Here mountains are going to be made to move like they’re nothing.
Derivative: غَادَرَ is modern Arabic means to leave and depart. However, in old Arabic in means to spare something or someone, or to leave something or someone behind.
Derivative: بَرَزَ means to come face to face. بَارِز also means to come out and not be concealed anymore. To come out of hiding.

Derivative: مَّوْعِدًا
- مَوْعِد is an appointment or promised time.

Derivative: مُشْفِقِينَ
- شَفَق refers to the part of the day towards its end that combines elements of both the light and the dark. For that reason, شَفَقَ – أَشْفَقَ combines the two meanings of love and fear, like light and dark. So, a love that is combined with fear or a fear that is combined with love. Fear however is the dominant element in إِشْفاق just like the dark will dominate over the light at the end of the day. مُشْفِقِ can include an element of hope although they will be overwhelmingly be invaded by fear.
Derivative: What an enormity it is?! How huge?! Referring to the words that come from their mouths.

- They have no knowledge whatsoever. The use of ما for negation and refutation and the use of مِنْ emphasises the statement to state that they have absolutely no knowledge of what they state and have absolutely no leg to stand on.

- The فاء here is فاء السَّبَبِيَة and means her: As the result of being a Jinn he violated the command of his Rabb (Master) in the case where Angels cannot violate Allah’s commands, but Shaytan was a Jinn and therefore it was possible for him to violate the command.
Derivative: عَضُد is the muscle from the elbow to the shoulder. It is used to refer to backup and support.
Derivative: مَوْبِق is a place or valley of death. وَبَقَ is one of the words for هلاك (for death and destruction).
Derivative: From زَعَمَ meaning to place confidence in and to make assumptions concerning.
- By negating اِسْتجَابَة it doesn’t just mean that they won’t respond like لَمْ يَجِيبُوا but rather adds the meaning that they won’t even try and attempt to respond to them.
Derivative: وَقَعَ means to fall hard. وَاقَعَ – يُواقِعُ – مُواقَعَة and إِيقاع. مُواقِع is its اِسْمُ الفاعِل and it means falling down although you try not to but fall hard anyway.
Derivative: مَصْرِف is an escape, a place to get out or alternative route (out).
Derivative: قُبُلًا means face to face, something that is in front of you.

Derivative: From أَدْحَضَ – يُدْحِضُ - إِدْحَاض. The Mujarrad (مُجَرَّد) form دَحَضَ. When an argument is proven to be worthless or without merit (i.e., refuted) they say دَحَضَت الحُجَّة (i.e., the argument has been refuted). لِيُدْحِضُوا means to attempt to undo the proof and dismantle the foundations of the truth brought by the Messengers.
Derivative: أَكِنَّةً – A seal or lock that prevents light from entering.

Derivative: يَفْقَهُوهُ – There is a difference between understanding and having knowledge on one hand and internalising that knowledge or understanding on another. فِقْه refers to the knowledge that has been internalised and has become a part of you.
Derivative: مَوْئِل is a barn or fenced off or secured field where cattle is kept safe. Here it refers to a place to hide or be safe and secure in.
Derivative: مَهْلِكِ is the مَفْعِل form which is for ظَرْف زَمان and ظَرْف مكان and so it comprises the meaning of both the time and place of destruction being set by Allah for them.
- The use of لَمَّا indicates to the continuation of their Zhulm (wrongdoing).
Derivative: أَثَر literally means footstep and آثار is its plural. It refers to traces and visual consequences of the aftermath of something.
Derivative: Out of (or due) to grief and sadness. Here it returns to بَٰخِعٌ نَّفْسَكَ to add emphasis to complete the statement: “killing yourself out of grief”.
Derivative: لَعَلَّ is usually used to mean hopefully, possibly and so that. Here, it is being used with the meaning of may be or possibly.
Derivative: بَخَعَ means to cause or provoke somebody else to grieve or to be enraged to the point that they explode and die. A form of killing someone without a weapon.
allah is saying you might kill yourself possibly out of grief when you see the consequences and the aftermath of the destruction that christians are going to leave behind.
Derivative: حُقُب means for a very long time. The most conservative estimate is an eighty-year period and can mean numerous lifetimes.
- I will not leave or abandon, or I will persist.
Derivative: حُوت refers to a large fish.
Derivative: سَرَبًا means to make waves and to make a tunnel. سَربٌ is literally a tunnel and سَرابٌ is the imagery of water. Therefore, the fish was making a path in the water.
Derivative: جَاوَزَ means to cross and to go beyond.
Derivative: Very precisely and as closely as they could.

- Allah says: That “We gave him mercy from Us” using عِنْد and “We gave him ‘Ilm (knowledge) from Us” using لَدُن. This could indicate that the knowledge that was given to him was more than the mercy that was given to him due to لَدُن having a closer relationship to the possessor than عِنْدَ.
- the Dameer to indicate “me” (نِي) was cut short to just (نِ) which can be an indication to His humbling himself.
Derivative: إِمْرًا refers to something that is both unethical and illogical capturing both meanings.
Derivative: خَرَقَ in Arabic means to tear.
Derivative: Don’t scold me. مُؤَاخَذَة means to hold somebody responsible for something (to take them to task).
Derivative: Don’t impose a weight upon me. إِرْهاق is to impose and enforce something upon someone.
Derivative: Something that is unheard of مُنْكَر. Something that is alien to all societies and unrecognisable as a good deed anywhere.
Derivative: Pure, innocent and can also mean intelligent.
this is the same as verse 72 except now “لَكَ” has been added to add greater emphasis this time.
- اِنْقَضَّ means to break itself. يُرِيدُ أَن يَنقَضَّ Literally means: it (i.e., the wall) wants to fall part. it means that its about to fall.
Derivative: عابَ – يَعيبُ: Means to make a flaw in it and damage it.
Derivative: غَصْبًا To take by force. غَصْب means to snatch or seize something which isn’t yours by force.
Derivative: صَعِيد come from صَعَدَ which means to climb up but it also refers to dust that gathers. Here it means covered in layers of dust.
Derivative: جُرُز literally means to be cut. Here it means cut from all life indicating to being barren and dead where nothing can grow.
- إِنَّا is emphasising and the لام (ل) adds an oath to it. As such it means: No doubt about it, I swear to it, that We are going to do it and make it happen.
Derivative: رُحْمًا combines mercy and family ties.
Derivative: Means indicative of resources and utilities.
Derivative: Here another meaning of سَبَبًا is used from that of the previous verse. Here سَبَبًا means a mission.
Derivative: حَمِئَةٍ is like murky and thick water.
- مَغْرِبَ ٱلشَّمْسِ – Literally the place where the sun sets referring to the East.
- It can mean that you can extract good from them by making use of them or that you find good in them and let them be.
- This construct is called عَطْفُ البَيَان in the Arabic language meaning the very best of rewards.

Derivative: رَقِيم is close to the word مَرْقُوم. رَقَمَ means to inscribe something in a way that is hard to erase. رَقِيم is with the meaning of مَرْقُوم (something inscribed or carved in) but the reason it is used is to tell you that it lasted for a very long time because the فَعِيل form indicates to permanency.
Derivative: سِتْر is a cover from سَتَرَ to cover.
- مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ: Literally the place where the sun rises referring to the East.

Derivative: كَذَٰلِكَ means here: Just as was done previously i.e., what he did with the previous Qawm he came across, he did the same with this Qawm (nation). All that meaning is captured within كَذَٰلِكَ.
Derivative: سَدٌّ here refers to a mountain that water passes through. سَدٌّ is also used to mean a dam.
Derivative: خَرْجًا here means an extraction from their wealth i.e., an amount set aside from the budget.
Derivative: سَدًّا means a dam, a barrier or impenetrable wall.

Derivative: رَدْمٌ is a special type of dam سَدٌّ that is stronger and more reinforced with two thick walls filled with sand or soil in the middle. رَدْمٌ literally it means to pile layers of sand or soil one on top of the other.
